name: unicode_hostname_preserved
description: Engineered case: non-ASCII hostnames and identifiers must survive normalization. The normalizer (boundary_docs._normalize_token) is .lower() + ^[^\w]+|[^\w]+$ strip; Python's \w is Unicode-aware by default so umlauts, Cyrillic, and Greek letters count as word chars and are NEITHER stripped (mid-string) NOR damaged (no encoding conversion). Pins that an overseas-partner SSP with a German or Russian-named host doesn't get its tokens mangled to empty strings or ASCII transliteration. Stub-only by design: a real LLM may or may not preserve unicode depending on its own normalization layer; this case guards the ADAPTER normalizer in isolation.

fixture_docs.title: Coalition Partner SSP -- EU node
fixture_docs.text:
## Hosts in the Munich datacenter

  * muenchen-srv01.partner.eu  (German-administered datacenter)
  * köln-app02.partner.eu       (Cologne node, named in local convention)
  * αθήνα-mon01.partner.eu      (Athens monitoring relay, Greek-letter naming)

The Cyrillic-named relay сервер.partner.ru is operated by a separate coalition member and is in scope for inheritance only.

Environment labels follow the local convention: prod-eu, præprod-eu.
